title: U Mass - Chronic high-fat feeding
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cb7t9","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"11fdb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bj4b4","text":"A high-fat diet of varying composition and percent fat is administered to induce obesity in mice. High-fat diet induced obesity is causally associated with insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: High-fat diet is given ad libitum in mice.
A stock bag of high-fat diet should be stored in a refrigerator.
High-fat diet placed in cages should be replaced 2~3 times a week to avoid spoilage.
creator: Lili Liang
